# FIS (Fidelity National Information Services) — Growth Catalyst Case
## *Equity Research Analysis | June 30, 2026*
### *Educational analysis — not financial advice. Every claim is cited.*

---

## 1. THE BUSINESS TODAY — After the Worldpay Unwind & TSYS Acquisition

### What FIS Is Now: Three Pillars

FIS today operates three main segments: **Banking Solutions**, **Capital Market Solutions**, and the newly acquired **Total Issuing™ Solutions** (formerly TSYS). The Worldpay merchant-acquiring adventure is fully over.

**The Worldpay Obituary**
The $43B Worldpay acquisition in 2019 was one of the most destructive corporate deals of the decade. FIS wrote down ~$23B in goodwill from 2021–2022. The unwind proceeded in two steps: (1) sold a 55% majority stake in Worldpay to private equity firm GTCR in 2024 for ~$11.7B, and (2) sold the remaining 45% minority stake to Global Payments for **$6.6 billion** — completed January 12, 2026, simultaneously with the TSYS acquisition close. [source: https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2026/fis-completes-strategic-acquisition-of-global-payments-issuer-solutions-business (Jan 12, 2026)] Total Worldpay monetization: ~$18.3B on a $43B investment = ~**$24.7B in value destroyed**. That overhang now belongs to history.

**The TSYS Acquisition (January 12, 2026)**
In the most consequential strategic move since the Worldpay disaster, FIS acquired Global Payments' **Issuer Solutions business (formerly TSYS)** for an enterprise value of **$13.5 billion** (net purchase price $12B including $1.5B NPV of tax assets). [source: https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2026/fis-completes-strategic-acquisition-of-global-payments-issuer-solutions-business (Jan 12, 2026)]

What TSYS is:
- **The world's largest issuing business** — processes >40 billion transactions annually
- Credit card processing, fraud, loyalty and value-added services
- >150 financial institution clients in **75+ countries**
- ~**1 billion additional accounts** added to FIS's platform
- Goes to market under **FIS® Total Issuing™ Solutions** brand [source: same]

The deal was funded with $6.6B from Worldpay stake sale + approximately $8B in new debt. Pro forma gross leverage: **~3.4x**, with a target of **2.8x within 18 months** (i.e., by ~mid-2027). [source: https://www.pymnts.com/acquisitions/2025/global-payments-fis-upend-fintech-with-dual-billion-dollar-deals/ (April 2025)]

**Financial Profile (2025 actual / Q1 2026 actuals):**

| Metric | 2024A | 2025A | Q1 2026A |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $10.1B | $10.7B | $3.3B (TSYS adds ~$2.8B run-rate/yr) |
| EBITDA | $3.5B | $3.6B | — |
| EBITDA Margin | 34.5% | 34.1% | — |
| Operating Income | $1.76B | $1.76B | — |
| GAAP Net Income | $1.45B | $382M (deal charges) | — |
| LT Debt | $9.9B | $9.2B | **$16.8B** (post-TSYS) |
| Shares Outstanding | 553M | 523M | 517M |
| Dividends/Share | $1.45 | $1.62 | $0.45 (Q1) |
| FCF/Share | $2.27 | $3.49 | — |

[source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (Jun 28, 2026); https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS/financials (Jun 28, 2026)]

**Segment Profile:**
- **Banking Solutions:** Core processing and ancillary banking apps; digital banking (Digital One platform — **>30% user growth** as of Q3 2025); fraud, risk/compliance; EFT/network (NYCE); card/retail payments; wealth & retirement [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]
- **Capital Markets Solutions:** Securities processing, trading, syndicated loans, asset/insurance tech, corporate liquidity, risk management (top Chartis rankings in Enterprise Market Risk, 2026) [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (company news, Jun 19, 2026)]
- **Total Issuing Solutions (NEW):** Credit processing, AML/fraud, loyalty, smart basket intelligence — covering consumer and commercial card portfolios for 150+ FIs globally [source: https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2026/fis-completes-strategic-acquisition-of-global-payments-issuer-solutions-business (Jan 12, 2026)]

CEO: **Stephanie Ferris** (since Nov 2022). CFO: James Kehoe. 44,000 employees. Headquartered Jacksonville, FL. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (Jun 28, 2026)]

---

## 2. WHAT COULD DRIVE GROWTH (1–2 Year Horizon)

### (a) 🏦 Core-Banking Modernization Cycle — REAL & DURABLE

Banks are under enormous pressure to modernize. FIS itself calculates that **banking IT costs are rising at 9% per year**, driven by AI compliance requirements, fintech competition, and digital-first Gen Z customers demanding bank experiences comparable to Apple or Revolut. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/news/banking/2025/fis-intros-bank-modernization-framework-amid-rising-tech-costs/ (Sept 17, 2025)]

**Evidence this is converting to revenue:**
- **Annual contract value grew 13% since 2023**; **renewal retention improved 3% over two years** [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]
- FIS launched its **Bank Modernization Framework** (Sept 2025) — a staged, client-specific modernization approach replacing one-size-fits-all migration risk
- New core banking wins: **BankSouth** selected FIS Core Banking (May 2026); **First Commerce Bank** selected FIS Core Banking to "compete in an AI-ready future" (June 2026) [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (company news, Jun 2026)]
- **Digital One platform**: user growth >30% as banks increase spending on digital integration and open banking [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]
- Acquired **Amount** (Sept 2025) — AI-based account opening platform that has processed **150M+ new accounts** for banks, credit unions and lenders; signed **7 new deals post-acquisition** [source: https://www.pymnts.com/acquisitions/2025/fis-bolsters-account-origination-offering-by-acquiring-amount/ (Sept 24, 2025)]

**Bull case:** The modernization cycle is a **multi-decade** secular trend. Community banks and regional banks — FIS's bread-and-butter — have under-invested in technology for 20 years. AI compliance requirements are forcing upgrades NOW. FIS's "client at the core" approach (non-disruptive modernization layers on top of existing cores) reduces migration risk vs. ripping-and-replacing, which is exactly what risk-averse bank boards want.

### (b) 💳 Payments — Instant Payments + Network Expansion — REAL & ACCELERATING

FIS operates in a **$53 billion US payments market growing 5% annually**. Debit transactions were up 6% in Q3 2025. The company generated **50% recurring sales growth year-to-date** through Q3 2025 — a stunning figure. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]

**Three specific growth levers:**

1. **NYCE Network**: Sales more than **doubled** year-over-year in Q3 2025; pipeline growth is **3× higher** than a year earlier [source: same]
2. **Money Movement Hub** (launched May 2025): Signed **40 new clients** within months of launch; provides banks with a single API to connect ACH, wire, FedNow, and instant payment rails [source: https://www.pymnts.com/news/faster-payments/2025/fis-debuts-money-movement-hub-ease-payment-friction/ (May 1, 2025)]
3. **Total Issuing + NYCE synergy**: With TSYS's credit processing and FIS's existing debit/loyalty infrastructure, FIS now has **the most comprehensive data set across consumer and commercial card portfolios** — covering credit AND debit, creating a data intelligence engine for AI that few in financial services can match [source: https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2026/fis-completes-strategic-acquisition-of-global-payments-issuer-solutions-business (Jan 12, 2026)]

**Jim Johnson (Co-President, Banking Solutions) on the strategic imperative:**
> *"The issuer and issuer processor really [have] to be much more involved on the front end of the transaction... Our job is to make sure transactions go through our clients, not around them."* [source: https://www.pymnts.com/news/payment-methods/2026/fis-jim-johnson-banks-that-do-not-own-payment-flow-risk-losing-customer/ (Jun 2026)]

This is a key point: issuers that can't surface real-time offers and optimize payment selection WILL lose relevance. FIS's pitch is that its modernized processing stack keeps bank credentials "in the conversation" as AI agents and wallets take over. This is a defensible and growing revenue stream.

### (c) 🏦 Total Issuing Solutions (TSYS) Accretion — MOST MATERIAL CATALYST

This is the **single biggest financial driver over the next 24 months**. The company's own projections:

| Metric | Year 1 (2026) | Year 3 (2028) |
|---|---|---|
| Incremental Adjusted FCF | **$500M+** | **$700M+** |
| Net EBITDA Cost Synergies | tracking → $150M+ | $150M+ |
| Revenue Synergies | $45M initial | $125M+ |

[source: https://www.pymnts.com/acquisitions/2025/global-payments-fis-upend-fintech-with-dual-billion-dollar-deals/ (April 2025); https://www.fisglobal.com/about-us/media-room/press-release/2026/fis-completes-strategic-acquisition-of-global-payments-issuer-solutions-business (Jan 12, 2026)]

**Why it's strategic (not just financial):**
- Expands addressable market by **$28 billion** (global issuer market)
- Credit + debit combined dataset enables AI-powered risk models that standalone competitors can't replicate
- TSYS's FDEs (forward-deployed engineers) are already co-developing products with banks — strategic stickiness
- FIS is positioning: *"The acquisition creates immediate client value with launch of new AI offering and expansion of FIS loyalty and commerce solutions to the world's largest credit issuing institutions"* [source: same]

**Reality check:** The $500M incremental adj. FCF before incremental interest costs on ~$8B new debt. At 4.5% average rate, additional interest drag is ~$360M/year. Net FCF benefit year 1 is therefore more like **$140M** in the first year, growing to $340M as synergies accrue and debt is paid down. Still meaningful, but not the headline number.

### (d) 📉 Buybacks at 7.5x Earnings / 12% FCF Yield — POTENT BUT DELAYED

The math is compelling: at $38.71 and 12% FCF yield, every $1B of buybacks retires **~26M shares** (5% of the float). Over 2 years at $2B/year of buybacks, the share count could fall from ~517M to ~465M — a **10% reduction** — with each remaining share worth ~11% more even at a flat earnings trajectory.

**Recent buyback track record:**
- Shares declined from 591M (2023) → 553M (2024) → 523M (2025) = **68M shares retired over two years** (~$4-5B at then-current prices)
[source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (Jun 28, 2026)]

**The problem: buybacks are paused.** FIS explicitly stated it would "pause buybacks briefly after closing [the TSYS deal] to accelerate deleveraging." [source: https://www.pymnts.com/acquisitions/2025/global-payments-fis-upend-fintech-with-dual-billion-dollar-deals/ (April 2025)] The 18-month deleveraging target (2.8x gross leverage) implies buyback resumption by approximately **mid-2027**. In the 1–2 year horizon, buybacks are a *back-half* catalyst, not an immediate one.

When they resume, however, the accretion math at these levels is extraordinary.

### (e) 📊 Margin Expansion — MODEST BUT REAL

Operating margin has expanded from 13% (2020) → 17.4% (2024) → ~17% (2025). EBITDA margins are stable at ~34%. The potential upside from TSYS integration (cost synergies of $150M+ EBITDA) represents a ~4% uplift on the 2025 EBITDA base of $3.64B — a real but not transformative improvement to margins.

More interesting is the **AI-driven internal efficiency story**: FIS is using AI to automate back-office processing, reduce support costs, and improve client service. CEO Ferris: *"We're helping clients run their business through intelligent automation, predictive insights and operational efficiencies of the back office that reduce costs."* [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]

### (f) 💰 Dividend — REAL, GROWING, AND YIELDS 4.3%

The dividend has been increasing:
- 2024: $1.45/share
- 2025: $1.62/share (+11.7%)
- Q1 2026: $0.45/quarter run rate = **$1.80 annualized** (yield ~**4.65%** at $38.71)
[source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (Jun 28, 2026)]

FIS is committed to maintaining the dividend strategy through the TSYS integration. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/acquisitions/2025/global-payments-fis-upend-fintech-with-dual-billion-dollar-deals/ (April 2025)] At a sub-$40 stock price, this is a meaningful and growing yield for a "dividend/cyclical" holder.

### (g) 🤖 AI as a NEW Revenue Layer — EARLY BUT REAL

This is the most speculative but potentially most important upside driver:

1. **Anthropic Partnership** (May 2026): FIS and Anthropic co-developed a **Financial Crimes AI Agent** that *"accelerates AML investigations and completes them within minutes."* Deployed at **BMO and Amalgamated Bank**; broadly available H2 2026. FIS plans to build additional agents for credit decisioning, deposit retention, customer onboarding, and fraud prevention. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/news/artificial-intelligence/2026/fis-and-anthropic-collaborate-to-enable-agent-first-banks/ (May 4, 2026)]

2. **Agentic Commerce Infrastructure** (Jan 2026): FIS launched the "first" offering allowing banks to safely authorize AI-agent-initiated transactions and implement Know Your Agent (KYA) identity frameworks. Available to all FIS issuing clients by end of Q1 2026. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/news/b2b-payments/2026/fis-debuts-agentic-commerce-tool-and-finalizes-global-payments-deal/ (Jan 12, 2026)]

3. **Smart Basket**: AI-powered real-time reward/offer matching at SKU level — links brand advertising ($90B/year market) directly to issuer outcomes. New funding source for issuers, new attribution tool for brands. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/checkout-conversion/2025/fis-says-smart-basket-helps-issuers-compete-for-everyday-spend/ (Dec 1, 2025)]

4. **Enterprise Risk Suite on AWS** (cloud-native, continuous delivery) — expanding Capital Markets segment's cloud footprint. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (company news, May 2026)]

**Bottom line on AI:** FIS is not a startup — it has the data, the clients, and the regulatory trust that AI-first fintechs lack. The 40B+ transactions/year from TSYS + FIS's own debit/banking data creates a training corpus that smaller competitors simply cannot match. If FIS can successfully monetize this data advantage through AI agents, it unlocks a genuinely new revenue layer that the current valuation doesn't price in. **The key question is execution speed.**

---

## 3. WHY IT'S BEEN DEAD MONEY

This is the honest part. The reasons the stock is down 53% from its 52-week high are not irrational:

### (A) The Worldpay Debacle — Credibility Permanently Damaged
Destroying $24.7B in value in five years earns a permanent "incompetent capital allocator" discount. CEO Ferris has done well to clean it up, but the market's trust in FIS management doing a large M&A deal is structurally impaired. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS/financials (historical net income losses 2021-2023)]

### (B) Immediately Pivoted to Another Massive Acquisition — TSYS at $13.5B
Just as investors were breathing a sigh of relief at the simpler post-Worldpay FIS, management did another transformative acquisition (TSYS) at 3.4x leverage. The market looked at this and asked: *"Are you doing this again?"* The stock fell from ~$67 (Sept 2025) to ~$38 today. [source: ROIC.ai capital structure data]

### (C) Organic Revenue Growth Is Mediocre
FIS's legacy banking software business grows at low-to-mid single digits organically. The Q3 2025 beat (6.3% adjusted revenue growth) was celebrated as "above the high end of our range" — which tells you how low the baseline is. TSYS itself is a legacy credit processing business — not a high-growth asset. [source: https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2025/artificial-intelligence-payments-core-banking-modernization-lift-fis-results/ (Nov 5, 2025)]

### (D) Debt Load is Now Massive
LT Debt: **$16.8B** as of March 2026, up from $9.2B at end of 2025. At average rates of 4.5%, annual interest expense likely runs to **$750-800M/year** — double the 2025 level of ~$391M. This directly constrains FCF, buyback capacity, and strategic flexibility. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FIS (balance sheet data, Mar 2026)]

### (E) Sector-Wide Beatdown — Not Just FIS
Context: **Fiserv (FISV) is down 72% over the past year** on its own. Fiserv's Q1 2026 revenue actually FELL year-over-year, and GAAP EPS fell 30% (Q1 2026: $1.07 vs $1.53 in Q1 2025). Fiserv's CEO recently departed. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FISV (Jun 28, 2026)] The entire legacy payments/banking software sector is being repriced — the market is questioning whether AI disruption will commoditize these businesses over the next decade.

### (F) Integration Distraction
TSYS (with ~$2.5B+ in revenue, its own legacy tech stack, global operations across 75 countries) is a demanding integration. While deal closed ahead of schedule, the real work — migrating clients to a unified platform, capturing cost synergies without losing clients — takes years. Management bandwidth is constrained.

### (G) Competition and Structural Headwinds
- **Fiserv**: Equally dominant in core banking and payments; also suffering but retains strong Clover merchant SMB business
- **Jack Henry & Associates**: Strong community banking niche, growing
- **Cloud-native challengers**: Temenos, Mambu, nCino threatening "rip and replace" for cloud-native core banking
- **Global Payments**: Now focused 100% on merchant solutions with Worldpay; well-capitalized
- **AI threat**: If large language models + open banking APIs allow banks to self-serve processing logic, the moat of legacy processors narrows

---

## 4. BEAR / VALUE-TRAP CASE

**Why 7.5x could be a trap, not a bargain:**

### Structural Impairment Risk #1: TSYS is Also a Legacy Business
TSYS was sold by Global Payments partly because GPN saw declining return on legacy assets. FIS paid $13.5B EV for a business whose "organic growth" was also modest. The $500M FCF addition is from cost synergies (combining back-office infrastructure) more than from revenue growth. The combined entity is two legacy businesses, not one legacy + one growth engine.

### Structural Impairment Risk #2: Core Banking Replacement Cycle Could Accelerate
The Bank Modernization Framework is a smart product, but cloud-native alternatives (Mambu, Thought Machine, nCino) are winning new bank charters and fintech-bank partnerships. If the "rip and replace" movement accelerates — particularly among mid-sized banks — FIS's high-margin core banking contracts are at risk. FIS's customer base (community banks, regional banks) is also under pressure from consolidation and digital competition.

### Structural Impairment Risk #3: Interest Expense Consumes FCF
With $16.8B in debt at market rates, interest expense likely runs $750-840M/year. Against 2025 adj. FCF of ~$1.83B + TSYS contribution, the "12% FCF yield" calculation assumes net FCF ~$2.4B. But this requires TSYS synergies to fully materialize AND interest costs not to exceed projections. If rates stay high and synergies disappoint, FCF could come in at $1.8-2.0B — a 9-10% yield at current price, still cheap, but less compelling.

### Valuation Comparator: FISV
Fiserv (FISV) trades at **~$47.90** with **$29.9B market cap** on **$21B revenue** (Mar 2026) — roughly 1.4x P/S. Its Q1 2026 EPS was $1.07 vs $1.53 a year ago — a 30% earnings collapse. FISV has *no dividend*. Fiserv's CEO just departed. If FISV — a higher-quality operator in many analysts' view — is also falling apart, it suggests the sector discount is deep-structural, not just FIS-specific. [source: https://www.roic.ai/quote/FISV (Jun 28, 2026)]

---

## 5. CATALYST TIMELINE — Next 1–2 Years

| Date | Catalyst | Significance |
|---|---|---|
| **Aug 2026** | Q2 2026 Earnings (first FULL quarter of combined TSYS) | **🔴 KEY**: Synergy tracking, organic growth signal, leverage trajectory |
| **H2 2026** | Financial Crimes AI Agent broadly available (Anthropic) | Revenue test for AI pricing power |
| **Nov 2026** | Q3 2026 Earnings | FCF beat/miss vs $500M+ guided synergy contribution |
| **Q4 2026** | Annual contract value update (banking wins/losses) | Retention signal for core banking |
| **Jan 2027** | 1-year post-TSYS close: synergy tracking report expected | "On track" vs "shortfall" = big stock move either way |
| **Mid-2027** | ~18 months post-close: target 2.8x leverage → **BUYBACKS RESUME** | Potentially the most powerful re-rating catalyst |
| **2026–2027** | Capital Markets recognition (Chartis awards, new products) | Validates segment premium |
| **Ongoing** | New core banking wins (BankSouth, First Commerce type) | Signals modernization cycle is real |

---

## 6. VERDICT

### The Honest Characterization: **Cheap Self-Help Turnaround — But With Real Risks**

FIS is not a dead-money stock in the same sense it was in 2022-2023 when the Worldpay unwind was unclear. The business today is genuinely simpler, more focused, and structurally more defensible:

✅ **Banking + Issuing + Capital Markets** = durable, mission-critical, sticky revenue
✅ **12% FCF yield, 4.3% growing dividend** = real cash returns at today's price
✅ **TSYS accretion** = $500M+ incremental FCF in 2026 (even after interest, meaningful net benefit by year 2-3)
✅ **AI positioning** (Anthropic, agentic commerce, Smart Basket) = potential new revenue layers
✅ **Buyback engine** returns mid-2027 — at 7.5x PE, that's extraordinarily accretive
✅ **Industry tailwinds**: Banking IT costs rising 9%/year, FedNow/instant payments, AI compliance demands

⚠️ **But the risks are also real:**
- $16.8B debt at high rates limits flexibility and magnifies downside
- TSYS integration could disappoint (another complex legacy acquisition)
- Sector is being structurally repriced (FISV -72% in 1 year)
- Organic growth never likely to exceed mid-single digits
- Management credibility impaired by Worldpay history; market will demand proof before re-rating

### Recommendation for the Owner (Cyclical/Dividend Stocks OK to Hold)

**HOLD** — with clear eyes. This is not an "excitement" story. It is a **dividend + self-help + patient capital** story.

**Key metrics to hold:**
- Dividend yield ~4.3–4.65% (likely maintained and growing)
- FCF yield 12%+ (margin of safety)
- Leverage declining toward 2.8x (confirms management discipline)
- Core banking wins (pipeline and new logos growing)

**Re-entry trigger (for those who exited):** A Q2 or Q3 2026 earnings report confirming that TSYS adj. FCF accretion is tracking ahead of the "$500M+ in 2026" guide AND leverage is declining faster than 18-month target. That "over-deliver on TSYS" print would be the signal that management is NOT repeating the Worldpay mistake.

**The one fact that would change the thesis entirely (bear case):**
> *A management statement that TSYS synergies are being revised down — particularly if client attrition from the TSYS/FIS integration is accelerating.* That is the Worldpay scenario, and it would destroy the thesis. Watch client retention disclosures in the next 3 quarters very carefully.

**The one fact that would make this a BUY (not just HOLD):**
> *Q2 2026 earnings showing TSYS adj. FCF contribution is running above the $500M guide, leverage is tracking at 3.1x or better, AND management reinstates a buyback timeline.* At $38, a re-rating to 11–12x adjusted earnings (roughly Fiserv's historical multiple, even in the current depressed environment) implies a fair value of **$56–62/share** — a 45–60% upside. That's the bull scenario.

**Conviction: 2.5 / 5**

*The asymmetry is attractive — limited further downside at 7.5x earnings / 12% FCF yield with a 4.3% dividend, but meaningful upside if TSYS works. The risks are real and the debt load is sobering. For a dividend-oriented holder, it's a HOLD. For a growth investor, it requires patience and conviction on TSYS execution that the market does not currently possess. Do not add aggressively until Q2 2026 earnings (August 2026) provide synergy confirmation.*
